title: Worker heartbeats and lost-worker handling
purpose: Keep long-running remote activities alive and detect dead workers. A worker heartbeats over its stream so the engine knows its task is still running; a worker that goes silent or disconnects before reporting has its in-flight task surfaced to the engine as failed, leaving the retry decision to the engine.
task: Implement heartbeat handling on the worker stream: track per-task liveness against a configured heartbeat window, accept Heartbeat messages (with optional progress), and when a worker misses its window or disconnects before reporting, mark it lost, remove it from the registry, and surface its in-flight task(s) to the engine as failed so the engine applies its retry policy. The window is configured, not hardcoded.

requirements.title: Heartbeat tracking
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL accept Heartbeat messages on the worker stream, recording per-task liveness against a configured heartbeat window, and SHALL accept optional progress in a heartbeat without treating it as a result.
acceptance: heartbeat.rs tracks, per in-flight task, the time of the last heartbeat and the configured heartbeat window (from AW-006 config, no hardcoded default — CO11).
A received Heartbeat (AW-005) refreshes the task's liveness; optional progress is recorded for observability but is not an ActivityResult and does not complete the activity.
A test feeds heartbeats and asserts a task stays live across the window.

requirements.title: Lost-worker detection and failure surfacing
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL mark a worker lost when it misses its heartbeat window or disconnects before reporting, remove it from the registry, and surface its in-flight task(s) to the engine as failed, leaving the retry decision to the engine.
acceptance: When a worker's last heartbeat is older than the configured window, or its stream disconnects with in-flight tasks, the worker is removed from the registry and each in-flight task is surfaced to the engine as a failed activity (D9).
The failure is surfaced via the engine's activity contract (as in AW-011 R3); the server makes no retry decision itself — the engine applies its retry/timeout policy (aion-engine D7, CO6).
The surfaced failure carries a terminal-or-retryable classification consistent with a lost worker (documented), so the engine's policy behaves correctly.

A test simulates both a missed-heartbeat timeout and an abrupt disconnect with an in-flight task, asserting the worker is deregistered and the engine's activity-failure path is invoked exactly once per in-flight task.

boundaries: SHALL NOT decide or perform activity retries — that is the engine's (D9, CO6).
SHALL NOT build the worker-side heartbeat sender — that is cluster AR.
SHALL NOT hardcode the heartbeat window (CO11).
verification: Time-dependent tests inject the clock; no wall-clock sleeps in tests.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
